papers_mined.title: Curling (Wikipedia)
papers_mined.note: Sweeping reduces friction via thin water film; transient melt; selective one-side broom controls curl.

papers_mined.title: Curling Stone Sweeping Effect (PMC)
papers_mined.note: Contact-pressure brush forms more water film; selective brooming alters trajectory.

papers_mined.title: Why does a curling stone curl? (Smithsonian)
papers_mined.note: Kinetic energy from sweeping → localized heat → thin water film → reduced friction.

papers_mined.title: Why Do They Sweep? Physics of Curling
papers_mined.note: Skilled sweeping adds 10-15 ft; transient localized melt.
